metadata.title: Agent Handoff
metadata.subtitle: Planner/evaluator transfer packet
metadata.description: A packet focused on multi-agent handoff: plan, evidence, unresolved risks, and figures share one auditable surface.
watchFor: The main plan should remain readable while evidence and risks negotiate around it.
Low-confidence unresolved risks should stay visibly warmer than the planner summary.
The figure should behave like supporting evidence, not a detached side panel.

guideSteps.label: Planner output
guideSteps.description: The planner summary is the stable center of this packet.

guideSteps.label: Evaluator evidence
guideSteps.description: Evaluator output should stay attached to the plan without flattening the rest of the packet.

guideSteps.label: Unresolved risk
guideSteps.description: This node marks what the next agent still needs to resolve.

tasks.title: Trace the handoff path
tasks.prompt: Follow plan -> evaluator evidence -> citation and verify the chain stays readable.

tasks.title: Inspect unresolved risk
tasks.prompt: Check the unresolved risk and its supporting figure without losing the plan summary.

claim.title: Agent Handoff Needs Co-Visible State
claim.statement: Multi-agent systems remain auditable when plan, evidence, and unresolved risk travel together instead of splitting across disconnected panes.

answerBlocks.text: The planner proposes the next action, but the evaluator and risk context must remain nearby so the handoff is inspectable.

evidence.text: The evaluator confirms that the proposed action preserves citation coverage and bounded scope.
The host bridge can attach annotations and selections without owning the actual layout.
Deterministic seeds allow the handoff scene to replay the same solved layout for audit.

contradictions.text: If the next agent cannot see why a risk stayed nearby, the surface stops being trustworthy.
